data.title: AI TRENDS | Zhipu's GLM Coding Plan Covers OpenClaw, Hermes, and SillyTavern
data.description: Zhipu product lead Zixuan Li announced that OpenClaw, Hermes, and SillyTavern are explicitly included in the support scope of the GLM Coding Plan. According to NS3.AI, Li emphasized that other general-purpose tools will be evaluated individually. He also cautioned users against sharing accounts or using subscriptions as APIs.

data.title: Zelensky stated that Ukraine is ready to hold talks with Russia in any form at any time.
data.description: Zelensky stated that Ukraine is ready to hold talks with Russia in any form at any time.

data.title: Binance launches Alpha page, showcasing Alpha Points and historical return data.
data.description: Binance has announced the launch of its Alpha page, showcasing the Alpha points system, historical Alpha event rewards, and progress data on related tokens' listing on Binance. This page will serve as a centralized entry point for Alpha-related information, allowing users to view historical performance and participation data.

data.title: Mexican Government Unaware of U.S. Involvement in Drug Lab Operation
data.description: The Mexican government was reportedly unaware of the participation of two U.S. officials in an operation aimed at dismantling a drug lab in northern Mexico. Bloomberg posted on X that the operation, which took place recently, involved collaboration between Mexican authorities and U.S. officials. However, the Mexican government was not informed about the involvement of the U.S. personnel in the operation. This incident highlights ongoing challenges in cross-border cooperation in combating drug-related activities. The lack of communication between the two governments raises concerns about the coordination and transparency of joint operations in the region.

data.title: Iran's Hormuz Strait Management Plan Includes Rial-Based Transit Fees
data.description: Iran's National Security Committee has outlined key provisions for managing the Hormuz Strait. According to Odaily, a member of the committee stated that one of the main clauses involves collecting transit fees in Iranian currency, the rial.

data.title: WLFI founder: Justin Sun's lawsuit against WLFI is baseless.
data.description: Zach Witkoff, founder and CEO of WLFI, stated that Justin Sun's recent lawsuit against World Liberty Financial is an attempt to divert attention from his own misconduct, and that his allegations are "completely unfounded." The company will seek to dismiss the case as soon as possible. He also stated that Sun's actions have prompted World Liberty to take measures to protect its own and its users' interests, and that the company will continue to take necessary actions to maintain community safety.

data.title: Projects such as OpenClaw and Hermes have been explicitly included in the GLM Coding Plan's support scope.
data.description: Zixuan Li, product manager at Zhipu, stated on the X platform that OpenClaw, Hermes, and SillyTavern are now explicitly marked as projects supported by the GLM Coding Plan. Other general-purpose tools will be analyzed on a case-by-case basis. Furthermore, Zixuan Li reminded users not to share accounts or use subscriptions as APIs. If users still encounter error 1313 while complying with the rules, they can contact the official support team for feedback.

data.title: Eric Trump: Justin Sun's lawsuit against World Liberty is "absurd," but not as outrageous as buying bananas for $6 million.
data.description: Eric Trump, son of Donald Trump, posted on the X platform, calling Justin Sun's lawsuit against World Liberty Financial "absurd" and saying it was "more absurd than spending $6 million on a banana taped to the wall." He also expressed "pride" in the World Liberty team.

data.title: Weatherford Anticipates Earnings Decline Amid Iran Impact
data.description: Weatherford, a leading global oilfield-service provider, has issued a warning regarding its earnings, indicating that the financial impact stemming from the situation in Iran is expected to worsen this quarter. Bloomberg posted on X that despite the anticipated downturn, the company remains optimistic about a potential rebound in the future. Weatherford's outlook reflects the broader challenges faced by the oilfield services sector amid geopolitical tensions and market fluctuations.

data.title: Iranian media: Key provisions of the Strait of Hormuz management plan include charging transit fees in Iranian rials.
data.description: According to the Iranian Students' News Agency, a member of the Iranian Parliament's National Security Committee stated that a key provision of the Strait of Hormuz management plan includes charging transit fees in the Iranian rial. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. crude oil inventories rose, fuel oil inventories fell, and total exports hit a new high.
data.description: The U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) said Wednesday that U.S. crude oil inventories rose last week, while gasoline and distillate fuel inventories fell, and total U.S. exports reached a record high due to supply disruptions caused by the war with Iran. The EIA said crude oil inventories rose by 1.9 million barrels to 465.7 million barrels in the week ending April 17, compared to an expected decrease of 1.2 million barrels. The EIA said crude oil inventories at the Cushing, Oklahoma delivery hub increased by 806,000 barrels. Despite the unexpected increase in inventories, oil prices still rose. (Jinshi)

data.title: Portugal Considers Air France-KLM and Lufthansa Bids for TAP Stake
data.description: Portugal is expected to move forward with competing bids from Air France-KLM and Deutsche Lufthansa to acquire a minority stake in its national airline, TAP. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the strategic interest from these major European carriers in expanding their influence in the region. The decision comes as Portugal seeks to strengthen TAP's financial position and enhance its competitive edge in the aviation market. The potential investment by either Air France-KLM or Lufthansa could provide TAP with the necessary resources to improve its operations and expand its network. The Portuguese government is evaluating the proposals to ensure the best outcome for TAP and its stakeholders.

data.title: Bitcoin May Have Found Market Bottom at $63,000, Says Grayscale Research
data.description: Grayscale Research has indicated that Bitcoin might have established a market bottom at approximately $63,000 as of February 5. According to NS3.AI, Zach Pandl noted that on-chain indicators reveal recent buyers have returned to profitability after Bitcoin's price surpassed their average purchase price of $74,000.

data.title: Bitcoin gains supported by short squeeze and U.S. demand
data.description: Bitcoin is experiencing a short squeeze dynamic, with steady demand from the U.S. contributing to its recent gains, according to CoinDesk. This market behavior is helping to sustain Bitcoin's upward momentum, as traders who bet against the cryptocurrency are forced to cover their positions, further driving the price higher. The ongoing demand from U.S. investors is playing a crucial role in maintaining Bitcoin's strength in the market.

data.title: BTC Reaches 11-Week High Amid Short Squeeze Potential
data.description: Bitcoin (BTC) has surged to an 11-week high, with dynamics indicating the potential for a short squeeze, according to CoinDesk. An analyst highlighted the building momentum for this market condition, which could further impact BTC's price movement.

data.title: Carrefour CFO: Limited Impact on Consumer Behavior Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: Carrefour's Chief Financial Officer has stated that the onset of the conflict in Iran has not led to significant changes in consumer behavior. According to Jin10, the CFO also mentioned that food inflation in France is expected to rise only slightly this year.

data.title: China's Economic Partnership with Iran Faces Limitations
data.description: China has emerged as Iran's leading economic partner, yet there are constraints to the extent of Beijing's support for Tehran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the complexities of this relationship. While China has been a significant buyer of Iranian oil, the partnership is influenced by geopolitical considerations and China's broader international relations. Despite the economic ties, Beijing is cautious about fully aligning with Tehran due to potential repercussions on its global standing and relations with other major powers. This cautious approach underscores the delicate balance China maintains in its foreign policy, prioritizing its strategic interests while engaging with Iran.

data.title: AAVE Founder: Multiple plans are underway to achieve orderly recovery of AAVE.
data.description: AAVE founder Stani posted on the X platform that the team is working with multiple partners to advance several solutions to achieve an orderly return to normal market conditions and ensure the best results for users. The Arbitrum Security Council has recovered $70 million worth of ETH, significantly reducing potential risk exposure. Various discussions and solutions are currently being considered. Stani stated that his focus is on Aave users and the protocol, and expressed gratitude for the support from industry developers. Further updates will be posted via @aave.

data.title: Trump Extends Iran Ceasefire by Only 3–5 Days as Internal Iranian Divisions Threaten Talks
data.description:
Key Takeaways
Trump has agreed to extend the US-Iran ceasefire for only 3 to 5 days, significantly shorter than markets had anticipated, to create a final negotiating windowPakistan says a second round of talks could resume within 36 to 72 hours, but Iranian officials say they have "not yet decided" whether to participate in Friday's talksThe IRGC is openly opposing the civilian government on key negotiating points, while Supreme Leader Khamenei's prolonged silence has left Iran without a unified decision-making voiceThe US is using the potential Strait of Hormuz blockade as a key pressure lever to force Iran back to the tableAnalysts warn that Iran's internal power struggle -- not US-Iran diplomacy -- is the biggest risk factor for a breakdown
The US-Iran ceasefire extension is far shorter than markets had priced in, with President Trump agreeing to extend the truce by only 3 to 5 days rather than the longer-term arrangement previously anticipated, according to multiple sources cited by BlockBeats on April 22.
The limited extension is designed to create a compressed final window for a new round of negotiations, reflecting the White House's urgency to reach a resolution -- or escalate -- rather than allow the conflict to drift into an open-ended ceasefire with no diplomatic progress.
Talks Uncertain as Iran Stays Silent
Pakistan, which has played a mediating role in the negotiations, said a second round of talks could resume as early as 36 to 72 hours. However, Iranian officials responding through state-linked Tasnim News Agency said they have "not yet decided" whether to participate in Friday's planned session -- a response that introduces immediate uncertainty into the already compressed timeline.
Iran's Internal Power Struggle Is the Core Risk
Analysts and US officials alike identify Iran's fractured internal power structure as the single biggest obstacle to a deal. The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, representing hardline factions, is openly opposing the civilian government on key negotiating issues and has gone as far as rejecting outcomes already agreed upon in prior rounds. Meanwhile, Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei's prolonged public silence has left Iran without a clear final decision-making authority, effectively paralyzing the country's negotiating posture.
The US assessment, per sources, is that a diplomatic agreement remains possible but that military options will be reactivated if Iran fails to present a unified position within the given timeframe.
Hormuz Blockade Remains the Key Pressure Tool
Washington is using the threat of a full Strait of Hormuz blockade as its primary bargaining chip, keeping maximum economic and energy market pressure on Tehran while talks continue. The strait handles approximately 20% of global oil supply, and any escalation that disrupts tanker traffic further would have immediate consequences for global crude prices and risk assets.
Crypto and Market Implications
The 3-to-5-day ceasefire window reframes the geopolitical timeline materially. Bitcoin's recovery above $78,900 and the broader crypto rally have been partly driven by improving peace sentiment following the ceasefire announcement. A compressed negotiating window with uncertain Iranian participation and active IRGC opposition introduces a sharp near-term risk: if talks collapse before a framework agreement is reached, the geopolitical risk premium that had been fading could rapidly return to energy and risk asset markets.
Traders should treat the Friday session as a binary event for near-term market direction.

data.title: U.S. Crude Oil Stockpiles Rise Amid Increase in Imports
data.description: U.S. crude oil inventories have experienced an uptick, driven by a surge in imports. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X that the latest data indicates a significant increase in stockpiles, reflecting changes in import patterns. This development comes as the global oil market continues to navigate fluctuating demand and supply dynamics. Analysts are closely monitoring these trends, which could impact oil prices and broader economic conditions. The rise in imports suggests adjustments in sourcing strategies, potentially influenced by geopolitical factors and market conditions. As the situation evolves, stakeholders in the energy sector are assessing the implications for future trade and pricing strategies.
